summary: Charter Addendum 3 is a coherent extension of Addendum 1's user-as-first-customer logic: Path A stranger credibility cannot survive an agentic-wizard UI, so the Fluent-class step-panel pivot is load-bearing for M8 dogfood. Decision-arc preserves V61-087/089/091/092 cleanly, scope discipline is strong, alternatives are real, and §3 HARD YES constraints have a credible Addendum-4 escape valve. Three P2 issues worth fixing before ratification: trigger framing stretches the autonomous_governance-rule-change category, Path-A-recruited-stranger has no engagement plan during the 6-10 week M-VIZ→M-AI-COPILOT wait, and line-A/line-B repo-location for the new 3D viewport infrastructure is undefined.

findings.title: Kogami trigger framing stretches the 'autonomous_governance rule change' analog
findings.problem: Per kogami_triggers.md (P-4), 'autonomous_governance rule change' is defined narrowly as DECs modifying RETRO-V61-001 / DEC-V61-087 / kogami_*.md. This Addendum modifies none of those — it changes WHAT the governance governs (product narrative + milestone routing), which is genuinely a charter-level pivot, not a rule change. Calling it the 'closest analog' is honest hedging but conflates two distinct categories. If 'charter-level pivot affecting milestone re-routing' is to become a recurring Kogami trigger, it should be named as such — either by adding an explicit trigger row to kogami_triggers.md §Must trigger, or by classifying this DEC under the existing 'phase-close' or 'high-risk PR' triggers (which also don't strictly fit). Letting the analog slide unnamed risks similar future Addenda being framed inconsistently.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) propose a successor DEC adding 'charter-level pivot / Pivot Charter Addendum' as a sixth Must-trigger row in kogami_triggers.md, OR (b) re-label the rationale in this DEC's frontmatter to 'charter-level pivot — closest existing trigger is high-risk PR by analogy on blast radius', NOT autonomous_governance rule change. Choose one and codify before Addendum 4 is ever drafted.

findings.title: Path A stranger engagement plan during 6-10 week M-VIZ→M-AI-COPILOT wait is undefined
findings.position: §Impact · Path A recruitment impact + §Failure modes row 4
findings.problem: DEC asserts 'Path A recruitment can run in parallel during this window — finding the stranger is independent.' This is mechanically true, but if recruitment succeeds in week 2 (per the 2026-04-27 recruitment plan's optimistic path), the recruited stranger then has 6-8 weeks of nothing to engage with — current SSE-wizard UI is what Addendum 3 just declared unfit for stranger demo. Strangers don't stay engaged in a 'we'll show you in 2 months' holding pattern; the recruitment relationship can decay silently. Failure-modes row 4 only addresses the case where recruitment STALLS, not the case where recruitment SUCCEEDS but the demo-ready UI doesn't exist yet.
findings.recommendation: Add a §Impact subsection or failure-mode row covering 'recruitment-succeeds-pre-M-PANELS' scenario. Concrete options: (a) async mockup-feedback engagement (Figma walkthrough of step-panel design before code lands), (b) early M-VIZ-only demo (3D viewport on imported geometry, no Fluent steps yet) as a interim handhold, (c) explicit 'we will re-engage when M-PANELS lands' communication policy. The Addendum should not silently assume strangers will tolerate an open-ended wait.

findings.title: 3D viewport infrastructure repo location is undefined relative to line-A/line-B isolation contract
findings.problem: ROADMAP §Line-A/Line-B isolation contract enumerates exact paths line-A owns: ui/backend/services/wizard_drivers.py, ui/frontend/src/pages/workbench/**, etc. M-VIZ introduces 3D viewport infrastructure (vtk.js / three.js / paraview-glance wrapper) that doesn't fit any existing line-A path: it's not under ui/frontend/src/pages/workbench/** (it's a viewport component used BY workbench pages), and M-RENDER-API backend rendering endpoints are not under ui/backend/services/wizard_drivers.py. Without a placement decision now, M-VIZ kickoff will spawn ad-hoc paths that may collide with line-B writes (e.g., src/visualization/ if such a path is later claimed by case-physics extractors).
findings.recommendation: Add a Scope clause: 'M-VIZ kickoff DEC must extend the line-A/line-B isolation contract in ROADMAP to declare the new viewport-infrastructure paths (likely ui/frontend/src/visualization/** + ui/backend/services/render/**) as line-A-only. The extension is a prerequisite for M-VIZ implementation, not a deliverable of it.' This locks in the boundary before the first viewport-infra commit lands.

findings.title: 60-day extension routes reframing as 'power-user advanced views' is asserted, not specified
findings.problem: DEC states WorkbenchTodayPage / WorkbenchRunPage / RunComparePage become 'power-user advanced views' that 'remain reachable but are no longer the primary interaction.' This is a significant UX reframe — those routes had real implementation investment (e.g., RunComparePage 349 LOC client-side diff per V61-088) and current navigation links them as primary surfaces. 'Reachable' is undefined: do they get a deprioritized nav entry? An advanced-toggle gate? Hidden behind a /pro/legacy/* prefix? Without specification, M-PANELS implementers will make ad-hoc choices that may break ongoing dogfood.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add one sentence specifying the reframing mechanism (e.g., 'remain at current /workbench/today, /workbench/run/{id}, /workbench/compare URLs but no longer linked from the primary step-panel shell — accessible only via direct URL or a future power-user nav drawer'), or (b) explicitly defer the reframing-mechanism choice to M-PANELS kickoff DEC with a non-negotiable: 'must not break current routes during M-PANELS implementation.'

strategic_assessment: Decision-arc coherence is strong: this Addendum extends Addendum 1's user-as-first-customer logic into the UI layer, which is the natural next step once Path A recruitment becomes the binding gate on M7/M8. The DEC correctly identifies that an SSE-wizard UI cannot survive a stranger demo and that doing M7/M8 first then redoing UI in M9 wastes dogfood goodwill. Roadmap fit is honest about the cost: 6-10 weeks delay on M7/M8 is real and the DEC doesn't try to hide it. Out-of-scope hygiene is exemplary — the explicit 'does NOT modify' list of 8 prior decisions / charters protects the established invariants (V61-087 governance, V61-089 two-track, V61-091 verdict cap, ADR-001 four-plane direction, Path A binding gate). Failure-modes table catches the most predictable regression (AI-处理 mis-implemented as auto-advance) and proposes a binding test. The §3 HARD YES constraints + Addendum-4 escape valve is the right rigidity-reversibility tradeoff for charter-level constraints. The arc is consistent with the project's pattern of letting CFDJerry's product-narrative criticism drive milestone re-routing under Addendum 1, which is itself ratified by the Pivot Charter. No re-litigation of accepted DECs occurred.
